name: Snubbin
concept: A pudgy hand-packed snow-hare who hugs his own belly to keep himself from crumbling, wearing the palm-prints of whoever packed him and a crooked carrot nose that's a size too big.
imagePrompt:
A collectible soft-vinyl toy figure of a chubby snow-hare called Snubbin, full body, standing upright and facing the viewer straight on, one single connected piece with a clean bold outer silhouette.

SILHOUETTE AND PROPORTIONS: an egg-shaped snowball body, widest at the belly, tapering slightly to a rounded top — head and body are one continuous mass with only a gentle pinch to suggest a neck. Two SHORT, THICK, ROUNDED ears that flop forward and fold over at the tips like the thumbs of woollen mittens — stubby and blunt, never pointed or thin, each ear as thick as his forearm. Tiny stumpy arms wrapped around his own round belly in a self-hug, hands (simple mitten-mitts, no fingers) clasped together at the front — this self-hug is the ONE idea: he is holding himself together so he doesn't crumble. Two squat conical hind feet planted flat and wide, giving him a low, stable, weighty base. A small round packed-snow ball of a tail tucked tight against the body. Head roughly 45% of total height. No thin strands, no whiskers, no fluff, no loose snow, no floating flakes — every part is thick, blunt, and attached.

MATERIAL: moulded soft-touch vinyl, the look of a high-end designer toy. Matte body with a subtle satin sheen along the top curves and a single soft specular highlight on the crown and belly. The surface is deliberately IMPERFECT the way packed snow is: broad, shallow, thumb-pressed dents and two clear scooped PALM-PRINT hollows on his back and flank, like a giant packed him by hand. Slight seam lines where the snowball halves were pressed together. Crisp moulded edges where the snow was patted flat on the feet. Everything reads at 4+ pixels — chunky, tactile, no fine texture.

PALETTE: body in cold packed-snow white, shading into soft periwinkle-blue in the undersides and belly shadow, with faint pale-lavender ambient occlusion in the creases. The ONE bold colour is a saturated CARROT ORANGE nose — a stubby cone of real carrot, slightly too large for his face and pushed in a little crooked, with two or three thick moulded ridges and a flat, blunt tip. Inner ears are a warm dusty rose. Cheeks have two small soft blush ovals of the same rose.

FACE: placed low and centred on the head. Two big, glossy, jet-black bead eyes, wide-set and honest, each with a single crisp round white catchlight and a smaller secondary glint — the eyes of a toy that trusts you. Small simple mouth: a soft moulded curve, content and close-lipped, just under the carrot nose. Brows are absent. The expression is patient, slightly worried, wholly lovable — the face of something small that is doing its best not to fall apart.

LIGHTING: clean studio product lighting, soft key from the upper left, gentle cool fill, warm bounce under the chin so the white never goes flat. Crisp readable form, high contrast between the pale body and the orange nose.
